Title: A Vacuolar Invertase CsVI2 Regulates Sucrose Metabolism and Increases Drought Tolerance in Cucumis sativus L.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: CsVI2 is predicted and experimentally supported to be a vacuolar, acid-type invertase and is located on chromosome 5 by annotation. It clusters phylogenetically with dicot vacuolar invertases and is clearly separated from fructosyltransferases, indicating it is not a member of the fructosyltransferase family. CsVI2 contains conserved β-fructosidase motifs (NDPD/NG), catalytic site WECVD and RDP motif, and multiple predicted glycosylation sites. Among cucumber VI genes, CsVI2, but not CsVI1 or CsVI3, is strongly induced by drought in young seedlings, suggesting CsVI2 is the major drought-responsive VI isoform. Structural modelling (not experimentally validated) suggests that the putative invertase inhibitor CsINVINH3 can bind to the active site region of CsVI2 and potentially inhibit its activity.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. CsVI2 encodes an acid-type vacuolar invertase with conserved β-fructofuranosidase motifs and specifically hydrolyzes sucrose into glucose and fructose in vitro and in vivo.
2. CsVI2 is expressed in multiple cucumber seedling tissues, with highest levels in the root elongation region, and its transcript abundance and vacuolar invertase activity are significantly induced by drought stress in young roots and leaves.
3. Overexpression of CsVI2 in cucumber seedlings enhances vacuolar invertase activity, decreases sucrose and increases hexose (glucose and fructose) contents in leaves, and confers increased drought tolerance compared with WT plants.
4. Phylogenetic and motif analyses place CsVI2 among dicot vacuolar invertases and separate it from fructosyltransferases; structural modelling suggests that the putative invertase inhibitor CsINVINH3 can bind to the CsVI2 active site region and may inhibit its activity.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CsVI2 functions as a vacuolar invertase whose overexpression increases vacuolar invertase activity, shifts leaf sugars from sucrose toward hexoses, and confers enhanced drought tolerance at the seedling stage.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in sucrose metabolism/carbohydrate partitioning under abiotic (drought) stress by promoting sucrose hydrolysis in the vacuole and increasing hexose availability, in coordination with drought-induced changes in SUS (CsSUS3) and SPS (CsSPS1) activities.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sucrose hydrolysis in the vacuole, converting sucrose into glucose and fructose as part of sucrose/hexose metabolism under drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Vacuolar invertase (acid β-fructofuranosidase) containing conserved β-fructosidase motif NDPD/NG, cysteine-containing catalytic site WECVD and RDP motif, with multiple predicted glycosylation sites.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuole, supported by signal peptide prediction and increased vacuolar, but not cell wall, invertase activity upon CsVI2 overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: CsVI2 is expressed in leaves, roots and stems of seedlings, with higher levels in young leaves and young roots; within young roots it is expressed in all regions but peaks in the elongation region. Under drought stress, CsVI2 transcript levels increase significantly in young roots from day 3 and in young leaves from day 6, paralleling increased vacuolar invertase activity.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed by water interruption on cucumber seedlings (sampling at 0, 3, 6 and 9 days) and two-week drought treatment used to evaluate drought tolerance of CsVI2-overexpressing lines.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought tolerance and sucrose metabolism in cucumber seedlings mediated by a vacuolar invertase.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought tolerance) and carbohydrate metabolism (sucrose/hexose balance).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought, CsVI2-overexpressing lines (especially OE-12 and OE-16) show significantly higher CsVI2 transcript levels and vacuolar invertase activities in leaves, accompanied by mildly reduced sucrose and significantly increased glucose and fructose concentrations compared with WT (p < 0.01 or p < 0.001). Vacuolar invertase activity in whole seedlings is significantly increased from day 3 of drought, together with induced CsVI2 expression.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Cucumis sativus cultivar 'HC-1' seedlings at the two-leaf stage; CsVI2-overexpressing cucumber lines OE-5, OE-12 and OE-16; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for transient transformation; Pichia pastoris strain X-33 expressing recombinant CsVI2 protein.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Cloning of CsVI2 CDS and overexpression under the CaMV 35S promoter; qRT-PCR analysis of CsVI2, CsSUS3 and CsSPS1 expression; heterologous expression and purification of recombinant CsVI2 in Pichia pastoris followed by in vitro substrate specificity and activity assays; transient expression of CsVI2 in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves to assay vacuolar and cell wall invertase activities; generation of stable CsVI2-overexpressing cucumber lines via cotyledon transformation; drought (water interruption) treatments of seedlings; measurement of vacuolar invertase, SUS and SPS enzyme activities; spectrophotometric determination of sucrose, glucose and fructose contents.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by heterologous expression of recombinant CsVI2 protein in Pichia pastoris (in vitro sucrose hydrolysis assays), transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves (compartment-specific invertase activity), and generation of stable CsVI2-overexpressing cucumber lines (OE-5, OE-12, OE-16) tested for vacuolar invertase activity, sugar contents, and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: CsVI2 was selected from previously screened putative vacuolar invertase genes in the cucumber genome and its full-length CDS (1893 bp) was cloned for functional characterization as an acid invertase-like, vacuole-localized candidate.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of CsVI2 enhances vacuolar invertase activity, increases hexose (glucose and fructose) accumulation, and improves drought tolerance in cucumber seedlings, suggesting CsVI2 as a candidate gene for developing drought-tolerant cucumber cultivars (e.g., via transgenic or gene-editing approaches) particularly targeting seedling-stage drought resilience.
